title: Bridge.ts type hygiene: rename payload variable, use Omit<> casts, fix EventEntry type
analysis: Task has 3 items. Items 1 (rename p→payload) and 2 (use Omit<> casts with imported interfaces) are ALREADY IMPLEMENTED in the current code — all 6 handlers use `payload` and cast via `Omit<TextDelta, 'type'>` etc. Only item 3 remains: change `EventEntry` payload type from `Record<string, unknown>` to `unknown` on line 167 of bridge.ts. This is a pure type-level change — no runtime behavior changes. The rationale is that each handler independently casts `event.payload as Omit<X, 'type'>`, so the container type should not constrain the shape. Using `unknown` is more accurate (prevents accidental uncast property access) and more permissive (doesn't assume payload is an object). The existing test suite in claudeBridge.test.ts has comprehensive runtime coverage (all 7 event types, rollback, extra fields, type override). The compile-time assertions at the bottom of that file verify Omit<> shapes. We add one small type assertion to document the `unknown` payload contract, then make the 1-line change.

steps.description: Add compile-time type assertion in claudeBridge.test.ts verifying that event handlers in subscribeToClaudeEvents are compatible with `{ payload: unknown }` event shapes. Add a comment block documenting that EventEntry's payload type should be `unknown` (not `Record<string, unknown>`) because handlers cast independently. This assertion will pass with both the old and new types (since `as` works from both), but documents the intent and will catch future regressions if someone narrows EventEntry's payload type incorrectly.

Change EventEntry payload type on line 167 of bridge.ts from `Record<string, unknown>` to `unknown`. This single-line change: `type EventEntry = [string, (event: { payload: unknown }) => void];`. All existing Omit<> casts compile from `unknown` source since TypeScript `as` assertions work from `unknown` to any type.
Run full test suite (`vitest run` in gui/) and typecheck (`tsc --noEmit` in gui/) to verify no regressions. All 15+ existing claudeBridge tests must pass and all compile-time assertions must hold.

design_decisions.decision: Use `unknown` instead of `Record<string, unknown>` for EventEntry payload
design_decisions.rationale: Each of the 6 handlers immediately casts event.payload via `as Omit<X, 'type'>`. The container type should not impose structure on the payload since the cast is the sole source of type narrowing. `unknown` is the correct TypeScript type for 'any value that will be cast immediately'. `Record<string, unknown>` incorrectly asserts the payload is always an object with string keys, which is an unnecessary constraint that could mask bugs.

design_decisions.decision: Items 1 and 2 from task description are already implemented — no changes needed
design_decisions.rationale: Current code already uses `payload` (not `p`) and `Omit<TextDelta, 'type'>` casts with imported interfaces from sidecar/src/types.ts. These were likely done as part of an earlier task. Only item 3 (EventEntry type) remains.

design_decisions.decision: Add type-level assertion rather than new runtime test
design_decisions.rationale: The change is purely type-level — it has zero runtime impact. The existing 15+ runtime tests in claudeBridge.test.ts already provide comprehensive coverage of all 7 event types, rollback on failure, extra field filtering, and type override protection. A compile-time assertion better documents the type contract.

reuse.what: Existing Omit<> type assertions in claudeBridge.test.ts (lines 464-471)
reuse.how: The file already has `Equals<A,B>` type-level assertion infrastructure and 6 Omit<> payload shape assertions. The new EventEntry assertion follows the same pattern.
TextDelta, ThinkingDelta, ToolCall, ToolResult, Done, ErrorMessage interfaces are already imported in bridge.ts and the test file. No new imports needed.
